skill.name: project-architecture
skill.description: Overview of the project's tech stack, directory structure, and architectural patterns.
skill.summary: Overview of the project's tech stack, directory structure, and architectural patterns.
skill.author: AbdulSamad94

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported backtick execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but all reviewed instances are Markdown documentation text in SKILL.md. No executable code, network behavior, secret access, filesystem mutation, prompt injection, or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Inline Code Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The reported locations are Markdown inline-code references to package names, directories, files, CSS paths, and npm commands. The skill file does not execute these strings, construct commands, or accept user-controlled command input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file is Markdown only, and the flagged backtick text is inline documentation. There is no code path that could execute a shell command.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Detection
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Line 3 is front matter describing project architecture. It contains no cryptographic algorithm, hashing call, key material, or security-sensitive implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact line is descriptive metadata. No cryptographic operation or weak algorithm appears in the skill file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Detection
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Line 31 describes a hybrid Next.js and Docusaurus application. It does not run host discovery commands, enumerate systems, or collect environment details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a human-readable architecture description. No evidence was found for system reconnaissance behavior.

content.value_statement: Teams need fast context on a codebase before making changes. This skill summarizes the documented stack, folders, integrations, and development entry points.
actual_capabilities: Summarizes the documented Next.js 15, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and Docusaurus stack.
Identifies the listed application folders and their stated responsibilities.
Explains documented authentication, database, ORM, and UI component choices.
Highlights the hybrid app pattern that combines Next.js and Docusaurus.
Points users to the documented development commands for root app, docs app, and combined startup.
limitations: It reflects only the architecture information written in SKILL.md.
It does not inspect source files outside the skill documentation.
It does not verify whether dependencies or commands still work.
It does not generate diagrams or enforce architecture rules by itself.

use_cases.title: Onboard a new engineer
use_cases.description: Provide a concise overview of the stack, folders, and development commands before the engineer starts implementation work.

use_cases.title: Plan a feature change
use_cases.description: Use the documented directory map to decide where app, auth, database, or documentation changes should begin.

use_cases.title: Review documentation structure
use_cases.description: Clarify how the Next.js application and Docusaurus documentation site are described as working together.

prompt_templates.title: Get the basic architecture summary
prompt_templates.prompt: Summarize this project's architecture in simple terms. Include the main stack, folders, and how to run the app.

prompt_templates.title: Find where to make a change
prompt_templates.prompt: Based on the documented architecture, tell me which folders are most relevant for an authentication change and why.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare onboarding notes
prompt_templates.prompt: Create concise onboarding notes from the architecture skill. Focus on stack choices, folder ownership, and development workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Assess architecture impact
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the documented architecture to assess the impact of adding a new authenticated dashboard page with related documentation.

output_examples.input: Summarize the project stack for a new teammate.
output_examples.output: This project uses Next.js 15 with TypeScript for the app, Tailwind CSS for styling, Better Auth for authentication, PostgreSQL with Drizzle ORM for data, and Docusaurus for documentation.

output_examples.input: Where should I look for authentication work?
output_examples.output: Start with the auth-related components, shared library files, and the documented auth client and server configuration. Check how sessions are shared with the documentation site.
The documentation lists separate commands for the root app and the docs app, plus a combined command for running both during development.

best_practices: Use the skill as a starting map before reading implementation files.
Verify documented commands and dependencies against the current repository before making changes.
Update the skill when architecture, folders, or development commands change.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the skill as proof that the implementation matches the documentation.
Do not use it as a substitute for security review of authentication or database code.
Do not assume undocumented folders or services are absent from the real project.

faq.question: Does this skill inspect the whole repository?
faq.answer: No. It summarizes the architecture details documented in the skill file.

faq.question: Can it explain the technology stack?
faq.answer: Yes. It lists the documented framework, language, styling, authentication, database, ORM, and documentation tools.

faq.question: Can it tell me where files belong?
faq.answer: It can point to the documented directories and their stated responsibilities.

faq.question: Does it validate development commands?
faq.answer: No. It reports the documented commands but does not execute or verify them.

faq.question: Is this useful for Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. It gives AI coding tools concise context about the documented project structure.

faq.question: Does it include security guidance?
faq.answer: Only at a high level. It does not review authentication, database, or deployment security.
